==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy monday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* A Japanese startup just challenged Anthropic's best AI models

* Z.ai is challenging the economics of frontier AI

* Tesla wants to sell AI infrastructure, not just use it

* Hyundai is betting big on robots

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **VIKTOR**

## [Show Viktor once. It just does it.](https://ref.viktor.com/vik-aiv-main4)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/d5e14d9c-819b-4e93-8699-a1e91c36d4e0/G8_3___A0004___GFT___Run_a_company_faster__easier__and_more_scalable.___Evergreen_TOFU___18.05.26___1200x628__3___1_.png?t=1782137932)
Follow image link: (https://ref.viktor.com/vik-aiv-main4)
Caption: Courtesy: Viktor

Viktor is the AI employee that lives in your Slack and Teams.

Show it how you handle reorders. How you draft launch briefs. How AP approvals move through your team. What your standup notes look like. Viktor learns each workflow once and starts doing it for you.

By week three, finance, marketing, eng, and ops are all delegating to the same employee. The question stops being "can Viktor do this?" and becomes "what should we hand off next?"

3,000+ integrations. 15,000+ teams. SOC 2 certified.

Get started free at [viktor.com](https://ref.viktor.com/vik-aiv-main4). $100 in credits, no card.

[Get started free →](https://ref.viktor.com/vik-aiv-main4)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1a606641-81c4-4928-93d2-af1223d25258/65a1d52837b6a1c4fad062f0_WhatsApp_20Image_202024-01-08_20at_2010.33.34_20AM__1_.png?t=1782139923)
Caption: Courtesy: Sakana AI

**1/ A Japanese startup just challenged Anthropic's best AI models:** Sakana AI launched [Fugu Ultra](https://x.com/SakanaAILabs/status/2068861630327443966?s=20), a multi-model orchestration system that dynamically coordinates specialized AI models behind a single API. Benchmark results suggest it performs on par with Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos across coding, reasoning, science, and agent tasks.

**2/ Z.ai is challenging the economics of frontier AI:** The company released [GLM 5.2](https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.2), an open-weights model designed for long-horizon coding, research, and agent workflows with support for up to 1 million tokens of context. Early evaluations place it alongside GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Opus 4.8 while costing significantly less to run and customize. You can access it from [Z.ai](https://Z.ai)[ chat app](https://chat.z.ai/) or [Z.ai](https://Z.ai)[ API](https://docs.z.ai/).

**3/ Anthropic's most popular model could be getting its biggest upgrade yet:** [Leaks suggest](https://x.com/i/trending/2068716608168587450) Claude Sonnet 5 may launch as early as next week with a 1 million-token context window, improved coding capabilities, stronger vision performance, and better handling of complex workflows.

**4/ Tesla wants to sell AI infrastructure, not just use it:** The company has filed a trademark application for "[Megapod](https://electrek.co/2026/06/21/tesla-megapod-ai-data-center-hardware)," a modular AI data center platform that bundles compute, networking, power, and cooling into a single system. If commercialized, it could position Tesla as a competitor to Nvidia in the rapidly growing AI infrastructure market.

**5/ Hyundai is betting big on robots:** Hyundai is reportedly [acquiring](https://x.com/lukas_m_ziegler/status/2068728984297234786?s=20) SoftBank's remaining stake in the robotics company, giving it full ownership of Boston Dynamics. The move could accelerate the deployment of robots like Atlas and Spot across Hyundai's global manufacturing and logistics operations.

* [Claude Design](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2067325887909884315) > Anthropic’s design tool now remembers your context and keeps every project consistent with your brand

* [Nex N2](https://nex-agi.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com) > A new family of AI models built for action-oriented tasks such as coding, search, and tool use

* [Kimi K2.7 Code](https://www.kimi.com/code) > Moonshot AI’s latest coding agent designed to handle complex developer workflows

* [Flex4DHuman](https://andy-cheng.github.io/Flex4DHuman) > Transforms a single video or a handful of camera views into multiple synchronized perspectives of a moving person

* [DreamX-World](https://amap-ml.github.io/DreamX_World) > An AI world model that generates fully explorable video environments rather than passive video clips

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjF9

PLUS: MONOLOGUE SHORTCUTS AND CORA OPENS ALPHA TESTING ON A FULL EMAIL APP TO REPLACE GMAIL https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2J1aWx0LW9uLW1vdmluZy1ncm91bmQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Every Staff https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BFdmVyeSUyMFN0YWZmIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9

Midjourney/Every illustration.

On June 12, Anthropic disabled Fable 5 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2ZhYmxlLWRpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9—the most capable coding model Every had worked with—after a U.S. government ban forced the company to shut it off for everyone. There was no warning or migration window. The models we rely on, it turns out, aren’t ours to keep: A company—or a government—can switch one off overnight.

That instability is the backdrop to our week. Senior applied AI engineer Nityesh Agarwal https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BuaXR5ZXNoIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9 spent weeks building around Claude’s limitations, only to watch Anthropic’s new dynamic workflows https://every. solve the problem overnight—the cost, he says, of working at the frontier. Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BsYXVyYV8yN2JiYWZfMSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo4fQ== and Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BrYXRpZS5wYXJyb3R0MTIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= report on loops https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2xvb3BzLWZvci1ub24tY29kZXJzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== (where you give an agent a goal and it works in rounds, reviewing each result and re-prompting itself until the work is good enough) moving fast from engineering into non-technical work. Head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0B0ZWRlc2NhdSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0= built an overnight NBA simulator and reworked Every’s subscription flow the same way; Katie’s half of the piece is a playbook for what to do when a frontier model disappears. Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BtaWtlXzIxMTQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTJ9 tried something stranger, building an AI persona https://every. of GitHub COO Kyle Daigle so the real conversation could focus on what no public record could tell him—which you can hear on this week’s AI & I https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3lvdXR1LmJlL09DRVZxeThrbDdRIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE0fQ==. And Stella Garber https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BzdGVsbGEuZi5nYXJiZXIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTV9, co-founder and CEO of Hoop, rebuilt an internal tool https://every. to be agent-native—then started shipping it to customers. Thursday’s edition rounds out the week with the AI topics the team has given itself permission to skip https://every., a workflow for treating your Slack bot https://every. like a coworker, and Mistral https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2hvdy1hbnRocm9waWMtbWFrZXMtY2xhdW

----------
==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy friday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* Midjourney's strangest bet yet

* OpenAI's next model may arrive sooner than expected

* Anthropic expects its most powerful models to be back online

* OpenAI is quietly becoming a healthcare company

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **REMIO**

## [The #1 Agent with all your context](https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/742d0790-5b27-45df-b49e-f9ca5634ba4f/remioads__1_.png?t=1781880591)
Follow image link: (https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Remio

No more copy-pasting. No more starting from scratch.

Just tell remio what you need — it already knows the rest. Write docs, generate reports, draft emails, create slides, all powered by everything you've ever worked on.

Your context. Your agent. Always ready.

[Try it here](https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **PEAK OF THE DAY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6ab5975c-25a0-4cbd-920a-b769fa473566/l271_35631781840967__1_.png?t=1781878734)
Follow image link: (https://x.com/midjourney/status/2067421950314688759?s=20)
Caption: Courtesy: Midjourney

## Midjourney's strangest bet yet

Midjourney just announced something nobody had on their 2026 bingo card: [a full-body ultrasound scanner](https://www.midjourney.com/medical/blogpost).

The scanner lowers people into water and surrounds them with ultrasound sensors before scanning the entire body in about a minute. Founder David Holz says it could approach MRI-level detail while being significantly faster and easier to deploy.

_But the hardware is only part of the story._

Midjourney also plans to open dedicated "[Midjourney Spa](https://www.midjourney.com/medical)" locations in San Francisco, combining the scanners with saunas, cold plunges, gyms, and hot tubs. Holz says the company hopes to build 50,000 scanners and eventually use the technology to help perform thousands of medical diagnoses.

For a company that built its reputation [generating AI artwork](https://www.midjourney.com/explore?tab=top), the move feels completely out of left field.

_Until you look around._

OpenAI is [creeping into healthcare](https://openai.com/index/improving-health-intelligence-in-chatgpt). Amazon built its own chips. Tesla went from software to robots. The biggest AI companies aren't staying in their lanes anymore.

Which makes Midjourney's scanner less surprising.

The real surprise is how quickly AI companies are turning into something else.

([See the announcement video here](https://x.com/midjourney/status/2067421950314688759?s=20))


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/776b5ffa-e05d-4ce6-b296-6622890969d0/HLG5xEebMAAc06M__1___1_.png?t=1781878602)
Caption: Courtesy: TestingCatalog

**1/ OpenAI's next model may arrive sooner than expected: **[Reports suggest](https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-prepares-gpt-5-6-models-for-the-upcoming-release/) GPT-5.6 could launch as early as next week, bringing improvements in coding, reasoning, agent workflows, and potentially a 1.5 million token context window. OpenAI is also rumored to be lowering prices again.

**2/ Anthropic expects its most powerful models back online within days: **Anthropic says access to Mythos and Fable 5 should [return soon](https://www.koreajoongangdaily.com/business/anthropic-confident-of-reenabling-mythos-fable-5-access-in-coming-days-executive/12727522) after temporary restrictions tied to security concerns forced the company to limit availability worldwide. The models remain available through a controlled-access program used by major technology companies.

**3/ The coding model race keeps accelerating: **Z.ai released [GLM-5.2](https://x.com/Zai_org/status/2066938937344495629?s=20), a model built specifically for large-scale software projects, debugging, research, and coding agents. With a [1 million-token](https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.2) context window, the model is designed to work across entire repositories rather than individual files.

**4/ OpenAI is quietly becoming a healthcare company: **OpenAI says more than [230 million people](https://openai.com/index/improving-health-intelligence-in-chatgpt) ask ChatGPT health-related questions every week. Researchers working with OpenAI also helped identify 18 previously unsolved rare-disease diagnoses that were later confirmed by physicians.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Goal Mode in Kimi Work](https://x.com/Kimi_Moonshot/status/2067574786965061677?s=20) > A persistent execution mode that keeps your desktop agent working around the clock until a task is completed, designed for long-running objectives, complex workflows, and multi-step projects

* [Perplexity Brain](https://x.com/perplexity_ai/status/2067642139014742348) > Now integrated into Perplexity Computer, Brain acts as a continuously learning memory layer that connects every task, interaction, and insight into a unified context graph

* [Ponder](https://x.com/timwangyc/status/2054207569523024251?s=20&utm_source=www.theaivalley.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=google-wants-to-replace-your-mouse) > The agentic video editor

Living at the edge of AI is bittersweet. You can spend weeks building a workaround to a problem only for a frontier lab to swoop in and solve it for you in a more elegant, reliable way. MINI-VIBE CHECK: DYNAMIC WORKFLOWS

A CLOSER LOOK AT HOW CLAUDE CODE COORDINATES MULTIPLE AGENTS

When senior applied AI engineer Nityesh Agarwal built Every’s AI project manager https://every. Claudie, he spent days figuring out how to get around the model’s limited context window, or the cap on how much text an LLM can process at once—and the reason Claudie kept dropping key details. His solution: one coordinating agent that delegated tasks to fleets of subagents https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3NvdXJjZS1jb2RlL2NsYXVkZS1jb2RlLWNhbXAiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=, which gathered data, made updates, and communicated with one another via local markdown files. The process was “a little bit hacky,” Nityesh says, but it worked.

If he were to build Claudie today, he could just use dynamic workflows https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2NvZGUuY2xhdWRlLmNvbS9kb2NzL2VuL3dvcmtmbG93cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMH0=, Anthropic’s feature for orchestrating large, multi-agent Claude Code tasks. Instead of deciding each step on the fly, Claude writes a reusable script that coordinates the work. It can assign tasks to many subagents and have them check each other’s work before reporting back the results.

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2F0dGlvLmNvbS8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1ldmVyeVx1MDAyNnV0bV9tZWRpdW09bmV3c2xldHRlcl9zcG9uc29yc2hpcFx1MDAyNnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1ldmVyeS1RMlkyNSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMSwiYWR2ZXJ0aXNlbWVudF9pZCI6MTE4Nn0=

AI can be exhilarating and destabilizing. Just when you think you have your setup figured out, a powerful new model drops https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9hbnRocm9waWMtbXl0aG9zLW91ci1mYWJsZS12aWJlLWNoZWNrIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9—or, in the case of Anthropic’s Fable 5, gets abruptly disabled https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZmFibGUtZGlzYWJsZWQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0=. Today, we explore this instability from multiple angles: Staff writer Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ== maps the grief (and coping mechanisms) that accompanied the Fable ban and shares a practical playbook for the next time a model you depend on disappears, head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQHRlZGVzY2F1IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== explains how loops are causing him to rethink his approach to working with AI, and GitHub chief operating officer Kyle Daigle tells AI & I https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcG9kY2FzdCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0= guest host Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMn0= how the company is responding to an agent-generated surge in commits.



----------------------------------------




‘AI & I’: CAN GITHUB BE FOR EVERYONE?

Today we’re releasing a new episode of our podcast AI & I https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcG9kY2FzdCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30=. Head of tech consulting Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNH0= guest hosted this week and spoke to GitHub COO Kyle Daigle about how the company is responding now that everyone—and their army of agents—can ship code.

The volume is extreme: Last year, there were 1 billion commits on GitHub. This year, that figure will safely exceed 14 billion, Daigle says, which puts GitHub in an important but delicate position: It must help developers handle agent-generated code without dictating which pull requests communities should trust or merge.

Watch on X https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5jb20vZGFuc2hpcHBlci9zdGF0dXMvMjA2NzI5Mjc3MTUyMjY1NDYyNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNX0= or YouTube https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veW91dHUuYmUvT0NFVnF5OGtsN1EiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTZ9, listen on Spotify https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vb3Blbi5zcG90aWZ5LmNvbS9lcGlzb2RlLzYyTkpUcnlVaDZEOGlkaGVSWkptMGU_c2k9TkVFNlV2UXpSeW0yam5hazdnRmJVZyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxN30= or Apple Podcasts https://every., or read the transcript https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcG9kY2FzdC90cmFuc2NyaXB0LWNhbi1naXRodWItYmUtZm9yLWV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE5fQ==. #### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

##### 1/ ChatGPT is about to sound a lot more human

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/10d60bb6-2da0-46d1-bc93-3a7e52db2a87/HK8qI4RbQAAQxSE__1_.png?t=1781704748)
Caption: 

OpenAI is preparing a major upgrade for ChatGPT Voice built around a new system called [GPT-Bidi](https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-prepares-major-chatgpt-voice-upgrade-with-gpt-bidi-1/). Unlike today's voice assistants, the model can listen and speak at the same time, allowing ChatGPT to handle interruptions, change its response mid-sentence, and carry on conversations that feel much more natural. References to GPT-Bidi have already appeared across OpenAI's apps, alongside new High, Medium, and Instant intelligence modes that let users choose between deeper reasoning and faster responses.

OpenAI is also [expanding Codex](https://x.com/jxnlco/status/2066970432855581052) with tools that can control desktop apps, interact with websites, and automate workflows across browsers and operating systems. Together, the upgrades push ChatGPT closer to becoming an assistant that can both talk and take action.

**Why it matters:**

Current AI voice assistants still feel unnatural. They pause, wait for their turn to speak, and often struggle when users interrupt them. GPT-Bidi is designed to remove those friction points and make conversations feel more like talking to another person.

Combined with OpenAI's growing push into browser automation and computer control, ChatGPT is moving beyond answering questions and toward completing tasks on a user's behalf.

##### 2/ A developer gave ChatGPT a robot body

Youtube: I Gave ChatGPT a Body (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S67z2aekBrI&utm_source=www.superpowerdaily.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=fix-this-code-the-three-words-that-led-the-u-s-government-to-ban-anthropic-s-fable-and-mythos)

A developer built a low-cost robot called [Growbot](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S67z2aekBrI&utm_source=www.superpowerdaily.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=fix-this-code-the-three-words-that-led-the-u-s-government-to-ban-anthropic-s-fable-and-mythos) by connecting a ChatGPT-like AI model to cameras, sensors, motors, and memory systems. The robot could interpret sensor data, describe physical sensations, write code, perform movements, and even mimic emotions. Memory also allowed it to learn from previous interactions and improve over time.

Despite those capabilities, Growbot still struggled with fine motor control and predicting the physical outcome of its actions. The creator believes the missing piece is a "world model," something similar to the human cerebellum that continuously predicts and adjusts movement in real time.

**Why it matters:**

For years, many people assumed better robots would require dramatically smarter AI. Experiments like Growbot suggest the opposite may be true. Modern language models can already reason, plan, learn, and communicate surprisingly well.

The harder challenge is helping machines understand how the real world behaves. Grabbing a cup, opening a door, keeping balance, or predicting the result of a movement remains far more difficult than generating text. The next breakthroughs in robotics may come from solving those problems rather than building larger models.
